# Content Style Guide

This guide defines the writing style for all AiVues pages. The audience is visually impaired people and the helpers and professionals around them. Most visitors use screen readers.

## Voice & Tone

- **Warm but direct.** Write like you're explaining something to a friend — not selling, not lecturing.
- **Second person.** Address the reader as "you." Use "they" for the glasses.
- **Present tense.** "The glasses describe what's in front of you," not "will describe."
- **Plain language.** Aim for reading level B1 (CEFR). Avoid jargon; if a technical term is needed, explain it on first use.

## Structure: Intro → Grouped Bullets → Closing

Every section follows this pattern:

1. **Short intro paragraph** (1–3 sentences). Sets context, explains *why* this matters.
2. **Grouped bullet lists** with a bold label line. Each group covers one purpose or theme. Keep bullets short — one line, no sub-bullets.
3. **Closing line or paragraph** (optional). Wraps up, tells the reader what to do next.

### Example

> Ray-Ban Meta glasses have a built-in camera and AI that you control with your voice. Think of them as an extra pair of eyes — not a replacement for your cane or guide dog, but a helpful addition.
>
> **Ask them and they'll tell you:**
>
> - What's in front of you — scenes, objects, colors
> - What a sign, menu, label, or letter says
> - Which direction to walk
>
> To get started you'll need the glasses, a phone, and someone to help with setup.

## Bullet Lists

- Use bullets for **scannable items** (features, steps, tips), not for running explanations.
- Group bullets under a **bold label line** that tells the reader what the group is about.
- Keep each bullet to **one line**. If it needs more, it should be a paragraph.
- **No sub-bullets.** Flatten or split into a new group.
- **No trailing periods** on single-phrase bullets. Use periods only when the bullet is a full sentence.

## Headings

- **H1** — page title only (one per page).
- **H2** — major sections. Always include a `{#slug}` ID for sidebar links.
- **H3** — subsections within a major section. Use sparingly.
- Headings should be short labels, not full sentences.

## Paragraphs

- Keep paragraphs to **1–3 sentences**.
- Lead with the key point. Details follow.
- Don't stack more than two paragraphs in a row without a heading, list, or other break.

## Bold & Emphasis

- Use **bold** for key terms, labels, and important warnings.
- Use *italics* sparingly — only for introducing a new term or light emphasis.
- Never bold an entire paragraph.

## Links

- Link text should describe the destination: "See [Koninklijke Visio](https://www.visio.org/)" — not "click here."
- External links get the full URL. Internal links use relative paths.

## Bilingual Rules

- Every content change must be made in both `/en/` and `/nl/`.
- Dutch text is a **localized rewrite**, not a word-for-word translation. Match tone and intent, adapt examples to the Dutch context where relevant.
- Voice commands to the glasses stay in English in both versions (e.g., "Hey Meta, what do you see?") because the AI responds to English prompts.

## Things to Avoid

- Walls of bullets without intro text.
- Marketing language, superlatives, or price comparisons.
- "Click here" or "see below" link text.
- Emoji in body text.
- Nested lists.
